snippet: // Signal 1: Resolution (stubbed to 1.0 until resolve is wired)
// Signal 5: Signal density — restatement suspicion (stubbed to 1.0)
// Signal 10: Source (local vs community — all local for now)
/// Incrementally update the index — for now, full rebuild.
//! Interactive terminal UI for exploring a Damask knowledge fabric.
//! Core data types for the Damask knowledge fabric.

payload.action: Wire damask_resolve::content_hash into rank_edge via the Freshness/Resolution types already defined in damask-core
payload.summary: Resolution signal stubbed to 1.0 — damask-resolve content hashing exists but is not wired into ranking. Spans are never checked for drift.

payload.action: Implement token overlap check (lint.rs already has a 60% threshold for restatement detection — reuse that logic here)
payload.summary: Signal density (restatement detection) stubbed to 1.0 — edges that merely repeat span snippets get full score instead of being penalized
Source signal always 1.0 — local vs community distinction not implemented. Low priority until multi-source ingestion is needed.

payload.action: Track file mtimes in index_meta table; only re-read changed JSONL files. Schema already has index_meta for this.
payload.summary: update_index() always does a full rebuild — drops and recreates SQLite from all JSONL. Will become a bottleneck as edge count grows.

payload.action: Extract shared row-mapping closures or a FromRow trait to reduce boilerplate across 10+ methods
payload.summary: ~500 lines of repetitive EdgeRow/SpanRow mapping boilerplate — every query method manually maps 8-9 columns. A row_to_edge/row_to_span helper would eliminate duplication.
10-signal composite ranking with hardcoded weights summing to 1.0. Well-tested (6 tests). Three signals stubbed. Weights not configurable but stable.

payload.action: Add tests for App state transitions in input.rs and data loading in app.rs — at minimum test that key events change active_view correctly
payload.summary: damask-tui has zero unit tests. All 6 views (Dashboard, Explorer, At, Graph, EdgeDetail, Log) and input handling are untested.
Active state computation: meta-edges (supersedes/invalidates) toggle is_active on content edges. If a supersedes edge is itself invalidated, the original is reinstated. Well-tested with edge cases.

payload.action: Consider adding lint config to DamaskConfig (thresholds section) for customizable rules
payload.summary: Lint thresholds hardcoded — 60% token overlap for restatement, 0.5 confidence floor, action required for risk/gotcha. Not configurable via config.json.

Foundation crate — pure types with no I/O. Span, Edge, Fact (tagged enum), IDs (ULID-based), Freshness, Vocabulary, PayloadEnvelope, Config. Comprehensive test coverage (~95%). This is the most stable layer.
Resolution signal is part of the 10-signal ranking composite (15% weight)
Signal density is part of the 10-signal ranking composite (5% weight)
Index rebuild is triggered before every query via update_index() — query performance depends on indexing speed
Closed — resolved, the underlying issue has been addressed

payload.action: One shared edge-render path (peek's staleness_marker pattern) used by every read command in both formats
payload.summary: Trust-signal coverage is inconsistent across read surfaces: at --format json emits spans WITHOUT resolution/recency (where --format json includes them), and search human output prints no staleness glyph and shows rank score in the same (x.xx) slot peek uses for confidence — the same edge renders '(0.90) ⚠' in briefing but '(0.98)' bare in search. Agents on the machine path or search path lose the anchor-freshness signal that is damask's core differentiator.

Closed: resolved: output/render.rs is now the canonical trust-signal path — at/search/where/orient/peek all source glyphs, signal cluster, and span/edge JSON from it; regression test trust_signals_uniform_across_read_surfaces locks it

snippet: pub fn update_index(db_path: &Path, edges_dir: &Path) -> Result<Connection, S...

payload.summary: Closed: resolved: signal density is no longer stubbed to 1.0 — it's computed from token_overlap_ratio and weighted 0.05 in the ranking composite (ranking.rs:102). Anchor (the stub comment) is gone because it was implemented.

Closed: anchor lost and structure evolved: signal_density is still 0.05 weight (ranking.rs:102) but the 10-signal numbering this edge references has since changed. Low-value structural note, self-evident in current code — closing rather than re-recording stale specifics.

Closed: resolved by retirement: damask-tui is mothballed (excluded from the workspace build, 2026-07-13). The untested-TUI risk is moot — the code is no longer in the shipped build. Visual browsing moved to the VS Code extension.

snippet: // Edges the current-state view must NOT contain: superseded (inactive)

payload.summary: compact resurrected closed edges (field-found by a Codex agent on Lia): the view removal set was built from !is_active only, but a CLOSED content edge stays is_active=1 (close ≠ supersede). So the view kept the content edge while dropping its  meta-edge (is_active=0), and ViewsPreferred reads then showed it OPEN again. Fixed: removal set is now (!is_active OR is_closed), so closed edges leave the view entirely and the view stays a faithful current-state snapshot. Views are regenerable, so deleting them to recover is always safe.
Closed: re-recording cleanly: summary lost a word to shell backtick evaluation

snippet: // Edges the current-state view must NOT contain: superseded (inactive)

payload.summary: compact resurrected closed edges (field-found by a Codex agent on Lia): the view removal set was built from is_active only, but a CLOSED content edge stays is_active=1 (close is not supersede). So the view kept the content edge while dropping its closed meta-edge (is_active=0), and ViewsPreferred reads then showed it OPEN again. Fixed: removal set is now (not is_active OR is_closed), so closed edges leave the view entirely. Views are regenerable, so deleting them to recover is safe.
